rawHead:
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Fixed-point theorems</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false,
      macros:{
        '\\Spec':'\\Spec',
        '\\Gal':'\\Gal',
        '\\Hom':'\\Hom',
        '\\tr':'\\tr',
        '\\ad':'\\ad',
        '\\ind':'\\ind'
      }
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>
<!-- breadcrumb-head-auto-begin -->

← Notebook
1 Banach 2 Brouwer 3 Schauder 4 Lefschetz 5 Kakutani 6 Applications

Fixed-point theorems

Four classical existence results — Banach, Brouwer, Schauder, Lefschetz — plus Kakutani's set-valued upgrade, organized by the structure they exploit: completeness, compactness, convexity, and homology.

blocks.html:
1. Banach contraction principle

If $(X,d)$ is a complete metric space and $f\colon X\to X$ satisfies a uniform Lipschitz bound $d(f(x),f(y))\le L\,d(x,y)$ with $L<1$, then $f$ has exactly one fixed point. The proof is constructive: pick any $x_0$, iterate $x_{n+1}=f(x_n)$, and the sequence is Cauchy with geometric decay $d(x_n,x_*) \le \tfrac{L^n}{1-L}d(x_0,x_1)$.

The two hypotheses are tight. Drop completeness and the iteration may flee the space; relax $L<1$ to $L\le 1$ (or even $|f'(x)|<1$ pointwise) and a fixed point can fail to exist — try $f(x)=x+e^{-x}$ on $\mathbb{R}$.

Picard iteration trajectory
Pick a contraction; watch $x_{n+1}=f(x_n)$ converge.
…
The cobweb diagram bounces between $y=f(x)$ and $y=x$. The horizontal step is $x_{n+1}-x_n$; the vertical step is $f(x_n)-x_n$. Slope $|f'|<1$ guarantees the spirals contract.

See also
  • Metric spaces

Used in
Fixed-point theorems · Applications across mathematics
Fixed-point theorems · Brouwer fixed-point theorem

2. Brouwer fixed-point theorem

Every continuous map $f\colon D^n\to D^n$ from the closed $n$-ball to itself has a fixed point. Equivalently, every continuous self-map of a non-empty compact convex subset of $\mathbb{R}^n$ has a fixed point. The theorem fails on the circle $S^1$ (antipodal map) and on the open ball (translation toward the boundary), so both compactness and contractibility matter.

The standard proof is a contradiction: if $f$ had no fixed point, the ray from $f(x)$ through $x$ would meet the boundary at a point $r(x)\in S^{n-1}$, defining a continuous retraction $r\colon D^n\to S^{n-1}$. But $H_{n-1}(D^n)=0$ while $H_{n-1}(S^{n-1})=\mathbb{Z}$, so the composition $S^{n-1}\hookrightarrow D^n\xrightarrow{r}S^{n-1}$ cannot be the identity — contradiction.

No-retraction lemma — the disk
Drag the test point to see the ray construction.
…
The blue dot is $x$, the violet dot is $f(x)$. The ray from $f(x)$ through $x$ hits the boundary at $r(x)$. A fixed-point-free $f$ would make $r$ continuous on all of $D^2$ — the contradiction. Drag $x$ inside the disk.

See also
  • Compactness
  • Connectedness
  • Singular homology

Used in
Fixed-point theorems · Applications across mathematics
Fixed-point theorems · Kakutani fixed-point theorem
Fixed-point theorems · Lefschetz fixed-point theorem
Fixed-point theorems · Schauder fixed-point theorem

3. Schauder fixed-point theorem

The infinite-dimensional generalisation: if $C$ is a non-empty closed convex subset of a Banach space $X$ and $T\colon C\to C$ is continuous with $T(C)$ contained in a compact set, then $T$ has a fixed point. The variant most used in PDE: a compact operator $T$ mapping a closed bounded convex set into itself has a fixed point.

Why compactness can't be dropped: in any infinite-dimensional Banach space the closed unit ball is non-compact (Riesz's lemma), and one can construct fixed-point-free continuous self-maps of it — Kakutani's example $T(x_1,x_2,\ldots) = (\sqrt{1-\|x\|^2},x_1,x_2,\ldots)$ on the closed unit ball of $\ell^2$ is the textbook witness.

Compact image of a convex set
Toggle whether $T$ is compact; watch the image shrink.
…
The yellow square is $C$. The shaded region is $T(C)$. When $T$ is compact, the image is contained in a small (precompact) set inside $C$ — Schauder forces a fixed point. Without compactness the image can escape any precompact neighborhood.

See also
  • Banach and Hilbert spaces
  • Compact operators and Fredholm alternative

4. Lefschetz fixed-point theorem

Let $X$ be a compact triangulable space and $f\colon X\to X$ continuous. The Lefschetz number is $$\Lambda(f) = \sum_{k\ge 0} (-1)^k\,\tr\bigl(f_*\colon H_k(X;\mathbb{Q})\to H_k(X;\mathbb{Q})\bigr).$$ If $\Lambda(f)\ne 0$, then $f$ has a fixed point. The implication is one-way: $\Lambda(f)=0$ is silent. For $f=\mathrm{id}$ the trace on $H_k$ is $b_k$, so $\Lambda(\mathrm{id})=\chi(X)$, the Euler characteristic — Lefschetz recovers the fact that a non-zero Euler characteristic forbids a fixed-point-free self-homeomorphism homotopic to the identity.

Brouwer is the contractible special case: on $D^n$ all higher homology vanishes, so $\Lambda(f)=\tr(f_*\colon H_0\to H_0)=1\ne 0$.

Lefschetz number computation
Pick a space and a self-map; read off $\Lambda(f)$.
…
$\Lambda(f)\ne 0$ forces a fixed point. The torus identity has $\Lambda=0$ but fixes everything — proof that the converse fails.

See also
  • Singular homology
  • Fundamental group

5. Kakutani fixed-point theorem

Replace single-valued continuous $f$ with a set-valued map $\varphi\colon C\rightrightarrows C$ assigning to each $x\in C$ a non-empty closed convex subset $\varphi(x)\subseteq C$. If $C$ is non-empty compact convex (in $\mathbb{R}^n$) and $\varphi$ has closed graph, then there exists $x^*\in C$ with $x^*\in\varphi(x^*)$.

The motivating example is Nash's existence proof: in a finite game each player's best-response set $B_i(\sigma_{-i})$ is non-empty compact convex but typically not a singleton when ties occur. The product correspondence $\sigma\mapsto\prod_i B_i(\sigma_{-i})$ on the product of mixed-strategy simplices satisfies Kakutani's hypotheses, so a fixed point — a Nash equilibrium — exists.

Set-valued map on $[0,1]$
Drag the threshold; the graph is a closed convex set.
…
$\varphi(x)=\{0\}$ for $xa$. The graph is closed, values are closed convex — Kakutani's hypotheses hold. The fixed point $x^*=a$ sits where the graph crosses the diagonal $y=x$, inside the vertical segment.

Used in
Fixed-point theorems · Applications across mathematics

6. Applications across mathematics

Every theorem above shows up as the existence engine in a downstream result:

  • Picard–Lindelöf for ODEs — Banach contraction. The Picard operator $T(\varphi)(t)=x_0+\int_{t_0}^t f(s,\varphi(s))\,ds$ is a contraction on $C([t_0-h,t_0+h])$ when $f$ is Lipschitz in its second argument and $h$ is small.
  • Implicit function theorem — Banach contraction. A Newton-style operator $y\mapsto y-D_yF(x_0,y_0)^{-1}F(x,y)$ is a contraction on a small ball, and its fixed point is the implicit solution $y=g(x)$.
  • Markov-chain stationary distribution — Brouwer. The simplex $\Delta\subset\mathbb{R}^n$ is non-empty compact convex; $\pi\mapsto\pi P$ is continuous; a stationary $\pi^*$ exists for any stochastic $P$.
  • Existence of solutions to nonlinear elliptic PDEs — Schauder. A compact solution operator on a closed bounded set in a function space provides existence without uniqueness.
  • Nash equilibrium — Kakutani. The best-response correspondence on the product simplex satisfies the hypotheses; its fixed point is the equilibrium.
Picard–Lindelöf in action
Iterate the Picard operator on $\dot{x}=f(t,x)$, $x(0)=x_0$.
…
Each iterate $\varphi_{k+1}(t)=x_0+\int_0^t f(s,\varphi_k(s))\,ds$ moves toward the unique solution, which is the contraction's fixed point.

Connections

Fixed-point machinery is the existence proof of last resort across analysis, geometry, and economics. The frontier today: variational and topological methods (Conley index, mountain-pass) for fixed points of non-compact maps on infinite-dimensional manifolds; computational degree theory; and equilibrium existence for general-equilibrium economies where Kakutani is replaced by Eilenberg–Montgomery or Browder.

  • Functional analysis — compact operators — the natural input class for Schauder.
  • Algebraic topology — singular homology — supplies the trace whose alternating sum is the Lefschetz number.
  • Point-set topology — compactness — the abstract hypothesis common to Brouwer, Schauder, Kakutani, and Lefschetz.
  • Partial differential equations — Schauder gives existence for nonlinear elliptic problems via a compact solution operator.
  • Dynamical systems — periodic orbits are fixed points of iterated maps, opening the door to Lefschetz–Nielsen counting.
